subject: hyperbus-sustained-bw-len128
notes: MEASURED on the physical Arrow AXC3000 (A3CY100BM16AE7S + Winbond W957D8NB HyperRAM) 2026-07-09 over USB-Blaster III. Single HyperBus burst of 128 words at CK=175 MHz (SDR PHY, byte clock 350 MHz), integrity ERR_COUNT=0 / RESULT=PASS. WRITE 308.97 MB/s (145 clk cycles), READ 285.35 MB/s (157 cycles). JTAG is control-plane only: the counters time the on-chip Avalon datapath, not JTAG access, so MB/s is the true HyperBus throughput (PLAN §8 method E respected). Bandwidth scales with burst length as the fixed CA+latency overhead (~17 clk WR / ~29 clk RD) amortizes. Bandwidth engine + SDR PHY from third_party/hyperram @ c6f5d2b; bitstream bw.sof sha256 8328e85b4e4e…; Quartus 26.1.0 Build 110. Peak of this sweep (len=768): 342.42 W / 337.26 R MB/s = the SDR PHY ceiling on this board; the 200 MHz/400 MB/s device max needs the DDIO PHY (blocked, see submodule README).
